zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Sir Richard de Spencer於1460年出生在英國英格蘭戴文可洛福利時，他的父親Edmund Spencer28歲，母親Joan Mallet25歲。他於1490年與Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer在英國英格蘭戴文結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1536年6月21日在英國英格蘭戴文享年76歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Spencer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): occupational name for someone who dispensed provisions or money, from Middle English spenser(e), spencer(e) ‘household steward, butler, almoner’ (Anglo-Norman French espenser, Old French despensier). Compare Spence and Spender . There is some dispute about the origins of the Spencer family, whose most famous member in recent times was the late Princess of Wales, born Lady Diana Spencer (1961–97). Some sources say that they are descended from William the Conqueror's steward, Robert Despencer. What is clear is that by the 15th century they had become prosperous from sheep farming in Northamptonshire. Robert Spencer (died 1627) was said to be the wealthiest man in England. Their titles have included Earls of Sunderland and Earls Spencer; and through the female line the 5th Earl of Sunderland also became Duke of Marlborough in 1733. This connection was the result of the marriage, in 1700, of the 3rd Duke of Sunderland to the daughter of John Churchill, 1st Duke of Marlborough. The youngest son of this union, John Spencer (1708–46), was the father of the 1st Earl Spencer (1734–83).

variants.name: Spence
meanings: English and Scottish: nickname for a servant employed in the pantry of a great house or monastery, from Middle English spense, spence ‘pantry, larder, storeroom’ (Old French despense, from a Late Latin derivative of dispendere, past participle dispensus ‘to weigh out or dispense’). Compare Spencer .

variants.name: Spender
meanings: occupational name or status name from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘steward, treasurer’, a shortened form of Middle English, Old French despendour, denoting someone who managed money or supplies. Compare Spencer .
alternatively, perhaps a nickname from Middle English spender(e), spendour ‘spender (of money), spendthrift’.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Old Norse, originally a byname meaning ‘bride, wife’; in mythology the name of Thor's golden-haired wife.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Sir Richard de Spencer was born in 1460, in Clovelly, Devon, England, United Kingdom, his father, Edmund Spencer, was 28 and his mother, Joan Mallet, was 25. He married Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer in 1490, in Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 21 June 1536, in Devon, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76, and was buried in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Sir Richard de Spencer est né en 1460, à Clovelly, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Edmund Spencer, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Joan Mallet, avait 25 ans. Il a épousé Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer en 1490, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 21 juin 1536, à Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterré à Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Sir Richard de Spencerが1460年にイギリスイギリスデボンクロベリーで生まれた時，父Edmund Spencerは28歳，母Joan Malletは25歳であった。1490年にイギリスイギリスデボンでLady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencerと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1536年6月21日にイギリスイギリスデボンで76歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアテウケスブリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Sir Richard de Spencer(이)가 1460년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 클로브리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edmund Spencer(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Mallet(은)는 25세였다. 그는 1490년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1536년 6월 21일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Sir Richard de Spencer родился в 1460 г. в местности клавелли, девон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Edmund Spencer было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Mallet, 25. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Margaret Elanoir Stuart de Spencer в 1490 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 21 июня 1536 г. в местности девон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.
